license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qiiduce
title: Density-based removal of bacteria from a culture of the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis
author: Sarah Duponchel

steps_raw.instruction: Determine the cell density of a Cafeteria roenbergensis culture: stain 10 µL of Cafeteria culture with 1 µL of Lugol’s acid iodine solution and count them on a haemocytometer (Neubauer Chamber)
Resuspend the cell pellet in 900 µL of 1X Gradient Buffer (0.5M NaCl, 1x PBS). Pipette several times to break up cellular aggregates
Centrifuge the Cafeteria cells for 5 min at 4,500 g, 20°C If it is possible use 50 mL Falcon-types to reduce cell loss
Dilute the Cafeteria culture to 5 x 10 5 cells/mL in f/2 medium + 0.03% yeast extract and let them grow O/N at 20-25°C
Under-layer the cell suspensions approx. 4 ml of 10% solution of OptiPrep using a syringe and a flat needle. The OptiPrep stock should be diluted in 1X Gradient Buffer
Load approx. 4 ml of 20% solution of Optiprep underneath the 10% solution using a syringe and a flat needle. The OptiPrep stock should be diluted in 1X Gradient Buffer
Centrifuge the tubes using an ultra-centrifuge at 20000 rpm, 20°C for 20 min with slow braking .
Recover Cafeteria cells from the gradient, at the interphase between the 10% and 20% Optiprep layers. You can collect them by pipetting from the top or use a syringe and needle.

Checked the samples under the light microscope for the presence of bacteria and to determine flagellate density. It is easier to dilute the collected samples in at least 10 ml of fresh f/2 media, before observing the cells. Depending on the bacterial populations, separation can not be as effective. You can use a gradient of OptiPrep (from 30 to 10%) to determine better conditions.
C. roenbergensis can be left in f/2 medium (no added yeast extract) overnight without affecting flagellate viability Account for about 30% of cell loss from the initial stock (from step 4)
In SW40 Ultra-Clear centrifuge tubes, load not more than 5 ml of the cells suspension in 1X Gradient Buffer.
It is recommended to remove the OptiPrep from the flagellates by centrifuging them at 4,500 g, 5 min, room temperature.
